10: # Skill Advisor IPC Helpers
11: 
12: <!-- sk-doc-template: code_folder_readme -->
13: 
14: ---
15: 
16: ## 1. OVERVIEW
17: 
18: `lib/ipc/` owns local IPC helpers used by the advisor launcher and daemon bridge. The code keeps secondary MCP clients on the existing advisor daemon instead of starting duplicate server processes.
19: 
20: ---
21: 
22: ## 2. OWNERSHIP
23: 
24: | Surface | Owner | Notes |
25: |---|---|---|
26: | Socket bridge | Skill advisor | Bridges secondary clients to advisor MCP server instances. |
27: | Idle monitor | Shared launcher pattern, advisor copy | Keeps advisor launcher shutdown policy close to the launcher package. |
28: | Lease files | Runtime state | Files under `database/` are ignored and must not be committed. |
29: 
30: ---
31: 
32: ## 3. KEY FILES
33: 
34: | File | Responsibility |
35: |---|---|
36: | `socket-server.ts` | Creates the Unix socket/TCP bridge for secondary MCP clients and exposes bridge stats. |
37: | `launcher-idle-timeout.ts` | Parses `SPECKIT_LAUNCHER_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MIN` and shuts down idle launchers. |
38: 
39: ---
40: 
41: ## 4. BOUNDARIES
42: 
43: - Keep transport, socket and idle-monitor behavior here.
44: - Keep graph database leases in `../daemon/` and `../skill-graph/`.
45: - Do not import handler-specific recommendation logic into IPC helpers.
46: 
47: ---
48: 
49: ## 5. ENTRYPOINTS
50: 
51: ```text
52: resolveIpcSocketPath()
53: startIpcSocketServer()
54: getIpcBridgeStats()
55: createLauncherIdleMonitor()
56: parseLauncherIdleTimeoutMs()
57: ```
58: 
59: Compiled consumers load these from `mcp_server/dist/mcp_server/lib/ipc/`.
60: 
61: ---
62: 
63: ## 6. VALIDATION
64: 
65: Run from the repository root.
66: 
67: ```bash
68: npm --prefix .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server run test -- tests/launcher-lease.vitest.ts
69: python3 .opencode/skills/sk-doc/scripts/validate_document.py .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/lib/ipc/README.md
70: ```
71: 
72: ---
73: 
74: ## 7. RELATED
75: 
76: - [`../README.md`](../README.md)
77: - [`../daemon/README.md`](../daemon/README.md)
78: - [`../../tests/launcher-lease.vitest.ts`](../../tests/launcher-lease.vitest.ts)
79: - [`../../../../../scripts/README.md`](../../../../../scripts/README.md)

(End of file - total 79 lines)

# Skill Advisor IPC Helpers

<!-- sk-doc-template: code_folder_readme -->

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

`lib/ipc/` owns local IPC helpers used by the advisor launcher and daemon bridge. The code keeps secondary MCP clients on the existing advisor daemon instead of starting duplicate server processes.

---

## 2. OWNERSHIP

| Surface | Owner | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Socket bridge | Skill advisor | Bridges secondary clients to advisor MCP server instances. |
| Idle monitor | Shared launcher pattern, advisor copy | Keeps advisor launcher shutdown policy close to the launcher package. |
| Lease files | Runtime state | Files under `database/` are ignored and must not be committed. |

---

## 3. KEY FILES

| File | Responsibility |
|---|---|
| `socket-server.ts` | Creates the Unix socket/TCP bridge for secondary MCP clients and exposes bridge stats. |
| `launcher-idle-timeout.ts` | Parses `SPECKIT_LAUNCHER_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MIN` and shuts down idle launchers. |

---

## 4. BOUNDARIES

- Keep transport, socket and idle-monitor behavior here.
- Keep graph database leases in `../daemon/` and `../skill-graph/`.
- Do not import handler-specific recommendation logic into IPC helpers.

---

## 5. ENTRYPOINTS

```text
resolveIpcSocketPath()
startIpcSocketServer()
getIpcBridgeStats()
createLauncherIdleMonitor()
parseLauncherIdleTimeoutMs()
```

Compiled consumers load these from `mcp_server/dist/mcp_server/lib/ipc/`.

---

## 6. VALIDATION

Run from the repository root.

```bash
npm --prefix .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server run test -- tests/launcher-lease.vitest.ts
python3 .opencode/skills/sk-doc/scripts/validate_document.py .opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/lib/ipc/README.md
```

---

99: function getMissingRequiredStringArgs(args: Record<string, unknown>, requiredKeys: string[]): string[] {

103:   });

127:     case 'skill_graph_query': {
128:       const missingKeys = getMissingRequiredStringArgs(args, ['queryType']);
129:       if (missingKeys.length > 0) {
130:         return validationError(name, missingKeys);
131:       }
132:       return toMCP(await handleSkillGraphQuery(args as unknown as Parameters<typeof handleSkillGraphQuery>[0]));
133:     }
134:     case 'skill_graph_status':
135:       return toMCP(await handleSkillGraphStatus());
136:     case 'skill_graph_validate':
137:       return toMCP(await handleSkillGraphValidate());
138:     case 'skill_graph_propagate_enhances':

142:   }
143: }

86: async function loadSkillGraphWatchFactory(): Promise<(paths: string[], options: Record<string, unknown>) => SkillGraphFsWatcher> {
87:   const workspaceRoot = resolveWorkspaceRoot();
88:   const candidates = [

90:     path.join(workspaceRoot, '.opencode', 'skills', 'system-spec-kit', 'mcp_server', 'node_modules', 'chokidar', 'index.js'),
91:   ];
92:   const chokidarPath = candidates.find((candidate) => fs.existsSync(candidate));
93:   if (!chokidarPath) {
94:     throw new Error(`Unable to load chokidar; checked ${candidates.map((candidate) => path.relative(workspaceRoot, candidate)).join(', ')}`);
95:   }
96:   const chokidarModule = await import(pathToFileURL(chokidarPath).href) as {
97:     default?: { watch?: (paths: string[], options: Record<string, unknown>) => SkillGraphFsWatcher };
98:     watch?: (paths: string[], options: Record<string, unknown>) => SkillGraphFsWatcher;
99:   };
100:   const watch = chokidarModule.default?.watch ?? chokidarModule.watch;

152:       console.warn(
153:         `[mk-skill-advisor-launcher] Skill graph post-index assertion failed: ${status.freshness}/${status.trustState.state}`,
154:       );
155:     }
156:   } catch (error: unknown) {
157:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
158:     console.warn('[mk-skill-advisor-launcher] Skill graph startup scan failed:', message);
159:   }
160: }

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Skill Graph Propagate Enhances Handler
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // MCP tool handler for skill_graph_propagate_enhances — detects and
5: // optionally applies missing inbound enhances edges across skills.
6: 
7: import { resolve } from 'node:path';
8: import type { MCPCallerContext } from '../../lib/context/caller-context.js';
9: import { requireTrustedCaller } from '../../lib/auth/trusted-caller.js';
10: import { errorResponse, okResponse, redactDiagnosticText } from './response-envelope.js';
11: import { propagateInboundEnhances } from '../../lib/cross-skill-edges/index.js';
12: 
13: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
14: // 1. TYPES
15: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
16: 
17: export interface PropagateEnhancesArgs {
18:   skillsRoot?: string;
19:   mode?: 'report' | 'propose' | 'apply';
20:   minConfidence?: number;
21:   targetSkillIds?: string[];
22:   sourceSkillIds?: string[];
23:   applyCandidateIds?: string[];
24:   applyAllHighConfidence?: boolean;
25:   dryRun?: boolean;
26: }
27: 
28: type HandlerResponse = { content: Array<{ type: string; text: string }> };
29: 
30: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
31: // 2. HANDLER
32: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
33: 
34: /** Handle skill_graph_propagate_enhances tool call */
35: export async function handleSkillGraphPropagateEnhances(
36:   args: PropagateEnhancesArgs,
37:   callerContext?: MCPCallerContext | null,
38: ): Promise<HandlerResponse> {
39:   try {
40:     // Trust gates the MUTATING path only: report/propose and dry-run apply
41:     // are read-safe and stay open to untrusted callers. This mirrors the
42:     // CLI's documented predicate (real apply = mode apply with dryRun not
43:     // true) exactly, so the two independent enforcement points agree.
44:     const mode = args.mode ?? 'report';
45:     if (mode === 'apply' && args.dryRun !== true) {
46:       const trustedCaller = requireTrustedCaller(callerContext, 'skill_graph_propagate_enhances (mode=apply)');
47:       if (!trustedCaller.ok) {
48:         return errorResponse(trustedCaller.error, trustedCaller.code);
49:       }
50:     }
51: 
52:     const cwd = process.cwd();
53:     const skillsRoot = resolve(cwd, args.skillsRoot ?? '.opencode/skills');
54: 
55:     // Workspace escape guard: resolved path must stay under cwd
56:     if (!skillsRoot.startsWith(cwd + '/') && skillsRoot !== cwd) {
57:       return errorResponse(
58:         `Refusing to scan outside workspace: ${redactDiagnosticText(skillsRoot)} is not under ${redactDiagnosticText(cwd)}`,
59:       );
60:     }
61: 
62:     const result = await propagateInboundEnhances({
63:       skillsRoot,
64:       mode: args.mode ?? 'report',
65:       minConfidence: args.minConfidence,
66:       targetSkillIds: args.targetSkillIds,
67:       sourceSkillIds: args.sourceSkillIds,
68:       applyCandidateIds: args.applyCandidateIds,
69:       applyAllHighConfidence: args.applyAllHighConfidence,
70:       dryRun: args.dryRun,
71:       writeIntent: 'automated',
72:     });
73: 
74:     return okResponse({
75:       skillsRoot,
76:       ...result,
77:     });
78:   } catch (err: unknown) {
79:     return errorResponse(
80:       `Skill graph propagate enhances failed: ${redactDiagnosticText(err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err))}`,
81:     );
82:   }
83: }

1: #!/usr/bin/env node
2: 'use strict';
3: 
4: const fs = require('fs');
5: const path = require('path');
6: const crypto = require('crypto');
7: const { spawnSync } = require('child_process');
8: 
9: const opencodeDir = path.resolve(__dirname, '..');
10: const mcpServerDir = path.join(opencodeDir, 'skills', 'system-skill-advisor', 'mcp_server');
11: const cliDist = path.join(mcpServerDir, 'dist', 'mcp_server', 'skill-advisor-cli.js');
12: const sourceHashState = path.join(path.dirname(cliDist), '.skill-advisor-cli-source-hash.json');
13: const defaultSocketDir = '/tmp/mk-skill-advisor';
14: const socketFileName = 'daemon-ipc.sock';
15: const allowStale = process.env.MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_CLI_DEV_ALLOW_STALE === '1'
16:   || process.env.SPECKIT_SKILL_ADVISOR_CLI_DEV_ALLOW_STALE === '1';
17: 
18: function fail(message) {
19:   process.stderr.write(`${message}\n`);
20:   process.exit(69);
21: }
22: 
23: function sourceCandidates() {
24:   const candidates = [
25:     path.join(mcpServerDir, 'skill-advisor-cli.ts'),
26:     path.join(mcpServerDir, 'skill-advisor-cli-manifest.ts'),
27:     path.join(mcpServerDir, 'tsconfig.json'),
28:     path.join(mcpServerDir, 'tsconfig.build.json'),
29:     path.join(mcpServerDir, 'schemas', 'advisor-tool-schemas.ts'),
30:     path.join(mcpServerDir, 'tools'),
31:   ];
32:   const files = [];
33: 
34:   const visit = (candidate) => {
35:     if (!fs.existsSync(candidate)) return;
36:     const stat = fs.statSync(candidate);
37:     if (stat.isDirectory()) {
38:       for (const entry of fs.readdirSync(candidate)) {
39:         visit(path.join(candidate, entry));
40:       }
41:       return;
42:     }
43:     if (candidate.endsWith('.ts') || candidate.endsWith('.json')) {
44:       files.push(candidate);
45:     }
46:   };
47: 
48:   for (const candidate of candidates) visit(candidate);
49:   return files;
50: }
51: 
52: function hashSourceFiles(existingSources) {
53:   const hash = crypto.createHash('sha256');
54:   for (const filePath of [...existingSources].sort()) {
55:     hash.update(path.relative(mcpServerDir, filePath));
56:     hash.update('\0');
57:     hash.update(fs.readFileSync(filePath));
58:     hash.update('\0');
59:   }
60:   return hash.digest('hex');
61: }
62: 
63: function readStoredSourceHash() {
64:   try {
65:     const parsed = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(sourceHashState, 'utf8'));
66:     return typeof parsed?.sourceHash === 'string' ? parsed.sourceHash : null;
67:   } catch {
68:     return null;
69:   }
70: }
71: 
72: function writeStoredSourceHash(sourceHash) {
73:   try {
74:     fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(sourceHashState), { recursive: true });
75:     fs.writeFileSync(sourceHashState, `${JSON.stringify({ version: 1, sourceHash })}\n`);
76:   } catch {
77:     // Freshness metadata is an optimization; stale detection remains conservative.
78:   }
79: }
80: 
81: function ensureFreshDist() {
82:   if (!fs.existsSync(cliDist)) {
83:     fail(`skill-advisor dist entrypoint is missing: ${cliDist}. Run the skill-advisor TypeScript build.`);
84:   }
85:   if (allowStale) return;
86:   const sources = sourceCandidates();
87:   if (sources.length === 0) return;
88:   const currentSourceHash = hashSourceFiles(sources);
89:   if (readStoredSourceHash() === currentSourceHash) return;
90:   const sourceMtime = Math.max(...sources.map((filePath) => fs.statSync(filePath).mtimeMs));
91:   const distMtime = fs.statSync(cliDist).mtimeMs;
92:   if (sourceMtime > distMtime) {
93:     fail('skill-advisor dist entrypoint is stale. Run the skill-advisor TypeScript build.');
94:   }
95:   writeStoredSourceHash(currentSourceHash);
96: }
97: 
98: function ensureSocketDir() {
99:   if (!process.env.SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR) {
100:     process.env.SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR = defaultSocketDir;
101:   }
102:   const socketDir = process.env.SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR;
103:   if (socketDir.startsWith('tcp://')) return;
104:   fs.mkdirSync(socketDir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
105:   const socketPath = path.join(path.resolve(socketDir), socketFileName);
106:   if (process.platform === 'darwin' && Buffer.byteLength(socketPath) > 103) {
107:     fail(`skill-advisor socket path exceeds the Darwin sun_path limit: ${socketPath}. Set SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR to a shorter directory or a tcp:// endpoint.`);
108:   }
109: }
110: 
111: ensureSocketDir();
112: ensureFreshDist();
113: 
114: const result = spawnSync(process.execPath, [cliDist, ...process.argv.slice(2)], {
115:   cwd: path.dirname(opencodeDir),
116:   env: process.env,
117:   stdio: 'inherit',
118: });
119: 
120: if (result.error) {
121:   process.stderr.write(`${result.error.message}\n`);
122:   process.exit(75);
123: }
124: 
125: process.exit(result.status ?? 1);

(End of file - total 125 lines)
</content>

  Line 125: ### Doc-Frontmatter Trigger Harvest (Flag-Gated)

  Line 127: With `SPECKIT_ADVISOR_DOC_TRIGGERS=true`, `skill_graph_scan` also harvests frontmatter (`title`, `description`, `trigger_phrases`, `importance_tier`, `contextType`) from every markdown file under each skill's `references/` and `assets/` (READMEs excluded) into a `skill_docs` table, and the watcher registers those docs so edits re-index the owning skill. Doc phrases score inside the `derived_generated` lane — top-3 docs per skill, tier-weighted, contribution capped at 0.45 — so they assist ranking but cannot hard-route alone. Matching recommendations carry an optional `matchedDocs` field (max 3 sanitized skill-relative paths) pointing at the exact doc to open. Flag unset (the default) changes nothing: no harvest, no watch targets, identical scoring. Deployment note: the launcher forwards only allowlisted env to the daemon child, so the flag works because it sits in `CHILD_ENV_ALLOWLIST` (`mk-skill-advisor-launcher.cjs`) — any future advisor env flag needs the same entry.


/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/ARCHITECTURE.md:
  Line 135: **Doc-trigger harvest (flag-gated).** With `SPECKIT_ADVISOR_DOC_TRIGGERS=true`, the scan harvests reference/asset doc frontmatter into a `skill_docs` table, the watcher tracks those docs for freshness, and the derived lane scores their trigger phrases (top-3 per skill, tier-weighted, 0.45 contribution cap), surfacing sanitized `matchedDocs` paths on recommendations. Default-off with byte-identical flag-off behavior; the Python shim harvests the same phrases under the same flag, and the launcher's `CHILD_ENV_ALLOWLIST` must carry the flag for it to reach the daemon child.


/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/changelog/v0.8.0.md:
  Line 1: This release gives the detailed frontmatter on skill reference/asset docs a real runtime consumer. With `SPECKIT_ADVISOR_DOC_TRIGGERS=true`, every `skill_graph_scan` harvests `title`, `description`, `trigger_phrases`, `importance_tier` and `contextType` from each skill's `references/` and `assets/` markdown into a new `skill_docs` table, the daemon watcher tracks those docs for freshness, and the scorer turns their phrases into dampened routing signal inside the existing `derived_generated` lane. Doc-specific prompts now rank the owning skill AND point at the exact file to open via an optional `matchedDocs` response field. The flag defaults off, and flag-off behavior is byte-identical to v0.7.0 — that invariance is the hard contract in the new test file.

  Line 11: - **`skill_docs` table** -- additive DDL (schema + migration), UNIQUE per skill+doc path, FK-cascade with skill deletion. The harvest runs for every skill on every scan — doc edits never touch `graph-metadata.json`, so node-level hash skips cannot gate doc work — and stays cheap via per-doc content hashes. Stale rows are swept per skill; unreadable docs warn (`DOC-READ-FAILED`) and continue.

  Line 18: - **`matchedDocs` on the strict schema** -- max 3 skill-relative paths, extracted from `doc:` evidence behind a sanitization allowlist (references/assets prefix, markdown only, traversal rejected); new `doc_reference_signal` evidence type.

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Advisor Scorer Lane Registry
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: 
5: import type { LaneDampingConfig } from './types.js';
6: import { ADVISOR_BM25_LEXICAL_SHADOW_LANE_ID } from './lanes/bm25.js';
7: 
8: const LANE_DEFINITIONS = [
9:   { id: 'explicit_author', defaultWeight: 0.42, defaultShadowWeight: 0.40, live: true },
10:   { id: 'lexical', defaultWeight: 0.28, defaultShadowWeight: 0.25, live: true },
11:   { id: 'graph_causal', defaultWeight: 0.13, defaultShadowWeight: 0.20, live: true },
12:   { id: 'derived_generated', defaultWeight: 0.12, defaultShadowWeight: 0.10, live: true },
13:   { id: 'semantic_shadow', defaultWeight: 0.05, defaultShadowWeight: 0.05, live: true },
14: ] as const satisfies readonly {
15:   readonly id: string;
16:   readonly defaultWeight: number;
17:   readonly defaultShadowWeight: number;
18:   readonly live: boolean;
19: }[];
20: 
21: export const SHADOW_SCORER_LANE_DEFINITIONS = [
22:   {
23:     id: ADVISOR_BM25_LEXICAL_SHADOW_LANE_ID,
24:     defaultWeight: 0,
25:     defaultShadowWeight: 0.10,
26:     live: false,
27:     envFlag: 'SPECKIT_ADVISOR_BM25_LEXICAL_SHADOW',
28:   },
29: ] as const;
30: 
31: export type ScorerLaneId = (typeof LANE_DEFINITIONS)[number]['id'];
32: 
33: const DEFAULT_WEIGHTS = Object.fromEntries(
34:   LANE_DEFINITIONS.map((lane) => [lane.id, lane.defaultWeight]),
35: ) as Record<ScorerLaneId, number>;
36: 
37: const DEFAULT_SHADOW_WEIGHTS = Object.fromEntries(
38:   LANE_DEFINITIONS.map((lane) => [lane.id, lane.defaultShadowWeight]),
39: ) as Record<ScorerLaneId, number>;
40: 
41: function resolveLaneWeightsOverride(
42:   envKey: string,
43:   defaults: Record<ScorerLaneId, number>,
44: ): Record<ScorerLaneId, number> {
45:   const raw = process.env[envKey];
46:   if (typeof raw !== 'string' || raw.trim() === '') return defaults;
47:   let parsed: unknown;
48:   try {
49:     parsed = JSON.parse(raw);
50:   } catch {
51:     return defaults;
52:   }
53:   if (typeof parsed !== 'object' || parsed === null || Array.isArray(parsed)) {
54:     return defaults;
55:   }
56:   const merged: Record<ScorerLaneId, number> = { ...defaults };
57:   for (const key of Object.keys(parsed) as ScorerLaneId[]) {
58:     if (!(key in defaults)) continue;
59:     const value = (parsed as Record<string, unknown>)[key];
60:     if (typeof value === 'number' && Number.isFinite(value) && value >= 0 && value <= 1) {
61:       merged[key] = value;
62:     }
63:   }
64:   return merged;
65: }
66: 
67: const RESOLVED_WEIGHTS = resolveLaneWeightsOverride(
68:   'SPECKIT_ADVISOR_LANE_WEIGHTS_JSON',
69:   DEFAULT_WEIGHTS,
70: );
71: const RESOLVED_SHADOW_WEIGHTS = resolveLaneWeightsOverride(
72:   'SPECKIT_ADVISOR_LANE_SHADOW_WEIGHTS_JSON',
73:   DEFAULT_SHADOW_WEIGHTS,
74: );
75: 
76: export const SCORER_LANE_REGISTRY: readonly {
77:   readonly id: ScorerLaneId;
78:   readonly weight: number;
79:   readonly shadowWeight: number;
80:   readonly live: boolean;
81:   readonly damping?: LaneDampingConfig;
82: }[] = LANE_DEFINITIONS.map((lane) => ({
83:   id: lane.id,
84:   weight: RESOLVED_WEIGHTS[lane.id],
85:   shadowWeight: RESOLVED_SHADOW_WEIGHTS[lane.id],
86:   live: lane.live,
87: }));
88: 
89: export const SCORER_LANE_IDS = SCORER_LANE_REGISTRY.map((lane) => lane.id) as [
90:   ScorerLaneId,
91:   ...ScorerLaneId[],
92: ];
93: 
94: export const LIVE_SCORER_LANE_IDS = SCORER_LANE_REGISTRY
95:   .filter((lane) => lane.live)
96:   .map((lane) => lane.id) as ScorerLaneId[];
97: 
98: export const DEFAULT_SCORER_LANE_WEIGHTS = RESOLVED_WEIGHTS;
99: 
100: export const DEFAULT_SHADOW_SCORER_LANE_WEIGHTS = RESOLVED_SHADOW_WEIGHTS;
101: 
102: export function isLiveScorerLane(lane: ScorerLaneId): boolean {
103:   return SCORER_LANE_REGISTRY.some((entry) => entry.id === lane && entry.live);
104: }

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Advisor Scorer Weights Config
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: 
5: import { z } from 'zod';
6: import {
7:   DEFAULT_SCORER_LANE_WEIGHTS,
8:   SCORER_LANE_IDS,
9:   SCORER_LANE_REGISTRY,
10:   isLiveScorerLane,
11: } from './lane-registry.js';
12: import type { ScorerLane } from './types.js';
13: 
14: export const EXPLICIT_AUTHOR_WEIGHT = DEFAULT_SCORER_LANE_WEIGHTS.explicit_author;
15: export const LEXICAL_WEIGHT = DEFAULT_SCORER_LANE_WEIGHTS.lexical;
16: export const GRAPH_CAUSAL_WEIGHT = DEFAULT_SCORER_LANE_WEIGHTS.graph_causal;
17: export const DERIVED_GENERATED_WEIGHT = DEFAULT_SCORER_LANE_WEIGHTS.derived_generated;
18: export const SEMANTIC_SHADOW_WEIGHT = DEFAULT_SCORER_LANE_WEIGHTS.semantic_shadow;
19: 
20: export const DEFAULT_SCORER_WEIGHTS = DEFAULT_SCORER_LANE_WEIGHTS as Readonly<Record<ScorerLane, number>>;
21: 
22: export const SCORER_LANES = SCORER_LANE_IDS;
23: 
24: export const ScorerWeightsSchema = z.object(Object.fromEntries(
25:   SCORER_LANE_REGISTRY.map((lane) => [lane.id, z.literal(lane.weight)]),
26: ) as Record<ScorerLane, z.ZodLiteral<number>>).strict();
27: 
28: export type ScorerWeights = z.infer<typeof ScorerWeightsSchema>;
29: 
30: export interface ScorerCalibrationThresholds {
31:   readonly confidenceThreshold: number;
32:   readonly uncertaintyThreshold: number;
33: }
34: 
35: export interface ReadOnlyScorerCalibrationProposal {
36:   readonly model: 'advisor-feedback-calibration-shadow-v1';
37:   readonly liveWeightsFrozen: true;
38:   readonly autoPromotion: false;
39:   readonly heldOutValidationRequired: true;
40:   readonly currentWeights: ScorerWeights;
41:   readonly proposedWeights: Readonly<Record<ScorerLane, number>>;
42:   readonly currentThresholds: ScorerCalibrationThresholds;
43:   readonly proposedThresholds: ScorerCalibrationThresholds;
44: }
45: 
46: export function parseScorerWeights(input: unknown = DEFAULT_SCORER_WEIGHTS): ScorerWeights {
47:   return ScorerWeightsSchema.parse(input);
48: }
49: 
50: export function liveWeightTotal(weights: ScorerWeights = DEFAULT_SCORER_WEIGHTS): number {
51:   return SCORER_LANES.reduce((total, lane) => {
52:     if (!isLiveScorerLane(lane)) return total;
53:     return total + weights[lane];
54:   }, 0);
55: }
56: 
57: function boundedUnit(value: number): number {
58:   return Number(Math.max(0, Math.min(1, value)).toFixed(4));
59: }
60: 
61: export function buildReadOnlyScorerCalibrationProposal(input: {
62:   readonly proposedWeightDeltas?: Partial<Record<ScorerLane, number>>;
63:   readonly currentThresholds: ScorerCalibrationThresholds;
64:   readonly proposedThresholdDeltas?: Partial<ScorerCalibrationThresholds>;
65: }): ReadOnlyScorerCalibrationProposal {
66:   const currentWeights = parseScorerWeights(DEFAULT_SCORER_WEIGHTS);
67:   const proposedWeights = Object.fromEntries(SCORER_LANES.map((lane) => {
68:     const delta = input.proposedWeightDeltas?.[lane] ?? 0;
69:     return [lane, boundedUnit(currentWeights[lane] + delta)];
70:   })) as Readonly<Record<ScorerLane, number>>;
71:   return {
72:     model: 'advisor-feedback-calibration-shadow-v1',
73:     liveWeightsFrozen: true,
74:     autoPromotion: false,
75:     heldOutValidationRequired: true,
76:     currentWeights,
77:     proposedWeights,
78:     currentThresholds: {
79:       confidenceThreshold: boundedUnit(input.currentThresholds.confidenceThreshold),
80:       uncertaintyThreshold: boundedUnit(input.currentThresholds.uncertaintyThreshold),
81:     },
82:     proposedThresholds: {
83:       confidenceThreshold: boundedUnit(
84:         input.currentThresholds.confidenceThreshold + (input.proposedThresholdDeltas?.confidenceThreshold ?? 0),
85:       ),
86:       uncertaintyThreshold: boundedUnit(
87:         input.currentThresholds.uncertaintyThreshold + (input.proposedThresholdDeltas?.uncertaintyThreshold ?? 0),
88:       ),
89:     },
90:   };
91: }

(End of file - total 91 lines)
</content>

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

28: } from '../schemas/advisor-tool-schemas.js';
29: 
30: type HandlerResponse = { content: Array<{ type: string; text: string }> };

32: const SKILL_ROOT = join('.opencode', 'skills');
33: const DEFAULT_MAX_METADATA_FILES = 5_000;
34: type SemanticLaneHealth = NonNullable<AdvisorStatusOutput['semanticLaneHealth']>;
35: 
36: function isFreshness(value: string): value is AdvisorFreshness {
37:   return value === 'live' || value === 'stale' || value === 'absent' || value === 'unavailable';
38: }
39: 
40: function parseDaemonPid(): number | undefined {
41:   const raw = process.env.SPECKIT_SKILL_GRAPH_DAEMON_PID;
42:   if (!raw) return undefined;
43:   const parsed = Number(raw);
44:   return Number.isSafeInteger(parsed) && parsed > 0 ? parsed : undefined;
45: }
46: 
47: function hasRunningDaemon(pid: number | undefined): boolean {
48:   if (!pid) {
49:     return false;
50:   }
51:   try {
52:     process.kill(pid, 0);
53:     return true;
54:   } catch {
55:     return false;
56:   }
57: }
58: 
59: function fileMtimeMs(path: string): number {
60:   if (!existsSync(path)) return 0;
61:   const stat = statSync(path);
62:   return stat.mtimeMs;
63: }
64: 
65: function resolveSkillGraphDbPath(workspaceRoot: string): string {
66:   // Resolve through the writer's own resolver so the integrity probe always
67:   // targets the exact file the daemon writes. An env override wins for both;
68:   // otherwise the workspace root stands in for the writer's cwd, which match
69:   // in the normal single-root deployment.
70:   return join(resolveSkillGraphDbDir(workspaceRoot), DB_FILENAME);
71: }
72: 
73: // Per-(dbPath, generation, mtime) integrity verdict cache. A full quick_check
74: // scans the artifact, which is too costly to run on the recommend hot path;
75: // caching it lets recommend opt into corruption detection while paying the
76: // scan at most once per generation. The only events that can change on-disk
77: // integrity — a generation bump or an mtime change — invalidate the entry.
78: const integrityVerdictCache = new Map<string, { ok: true } | { ok: false; reason: string }>();
79: 
80: function checkSqliteIntegrityCached(
81:   dbPath: string,
82:   generation: number,
83:   mtimeMs: number,
84: ): { ok: true } | { ok: false; reason: string } {
85:   const key = `${dbPath}::${generation}::${mtimeMs}`;
86:   const cached = integrityVerdictCache.get(key);
87:   if (cached) {
88:     return cached;
89:   }
90:   const result = checkSqliteIntegrity(dbPath);
91:   // Only the newest (path, generation, mtime) verdict is ever consulted, so a
92:   // single-entry cache is sufficient and self-bounding.
93:   integrityVerdictCache.clear();
94:   integrityVerdictCache.set(key, result);
95:   return result;
96: }
97: 
98: function tableExists(database: Database.Database, tableName: string): boolean {
99:   const row = database.prepare(`
100:     SELECT 1 AS present
101:     FROM sqlite_master
102:     WHERE type = 'table' AND name = ?
103:     LIMIT 1
104:   `).get(tableName) as { present: number } | undefined;
105:   return Boolean(row);
106: }
107: 
108: function metadataValue(database: Database.Database, key: string): string | null {
109:   if (!tableExists(database, 'vec_metadata')) return null;
110:   const row = database.prepare('SELECT value FROM vec_metadata WHERE key = ?').get(key) as { value: string } | undefined;
111:   return row?.value ?? null;
112: }
113: 
114: function countRows(database: Database.Database, sql: string): number {
115:   const row = database.prepare(sql).get() as { c: number } | undefined;
116:   return row?.c ?? 0;
117: }
118: 
119: function maxUpdatedAt(database: Database.Database, tableName: string): string | null {
120:   const row = database.prepare(`SELECT MAX(updated_at) AS updatedAt FROM ${tableName}`).get() as { updatedAt: string | null } | undefined;
121:   return row?.updatedAt ?? null;
122: }
123: 
124: function baseSemanticLaneHealth(disabledReason: string | null): SemanticLaneHealth {
125:   return {
126:     activeEmbedder: null,
127:     vectorCoverage: { embedded: 0, total: 0, ratio: 0 },
128:     dimMismatch: false,
129:     lastRefresh: null,
130:     disabledReason,
131:     laneEnabled: false,
132:     checkedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
133:   };
134: }
135: 
136: function readSemanticLaneHealth(dbPath: string): SemanticLaneHealth {
137:   const runtime = getSemanticShadowRuntimeHealth();
138:   if (!existsSync(dbPath)) {
139:     return baseSemanticLaneHealth(runtime.disabledReason ?? 'database_absent');
140:   }
141: 
142:   let database: Database.Database | null = null;
143:   try {
144:     database = new Database(dbPath, { readonly: true, fileMustExist: true });
145:     const total = tableExists(database, 'skill_nodes')
146:       ? countRows(database, 'SELECT COUNT(*) AS c FROM skill_nodes')
147:       : 0;
148:     const activeName = metadataValue(database, 'active_embedder_name');
149:     const activeDimRaw = metadataValue(database, 'active_embedder_dim');
150:     const activeDim = activeDimRaw ? Number.parseInt(activeDimRaw, 10) : 0;
151:     const hasActive = Boolean(activeName) && Number.isInteger(activeDim) && activeDim > 0;
152:     const adapter = activeName ? getAdapter(activeName) : undefined;
153:     const activeEmbedder = activeName
154:       ? { name: activeName, dim: Number.isInteger(activeDim) && activeDim > 0 ? activeDim : 0, adapterDim: adapter?.dim ?? null }
155:       : null;
156:     const tableName = hasActive ? `vec_${activeDim}` : null;
157:     const hasVecTable = tableName ? tableExists(database, tableName) : false;
158:     const embedded = hasVecTable
159:       ? countRows(database, `SELECT COUNT(*) AS c FROM ${tableName} WHERE embedding IS NOT NULL`)
160:       : (tableExists(database, 'skill_nodes') ? countRows(database, 'SELECT COUNT(*) AS c FROM skill_nodes WHERE embedding IS NOT NULL') : 0);
161:     const lastRefresh = hasVecTable && tableName ? maxUpdatedAt(database, tableName) : null;
162:     const dimMismatch = Boolean(activeEmbedder && activeEmbedder.adapterDim !== null && activeEmbedder.dim !== activeEmbedder.adapterDim);
163:     const disabledReason = dimMismatch
164:       ? 'dim_mismatch'
165:       : (!hasActive
166:           ? 'active_embedder_unset'
167:           : (!adapter
168:               ? 'adapter_unavailable'
169:               : (!hasVecTable
170:                   ? 'vector_table_missing'
171:                   : (total > 0 && embedded === 0 ? 'no_skill_vectors' : runtime.disabledReason))));
172:     return {
173:       activeEmbedder,
174:       vectorCoverage: {
175:         embedded,
176:         total,
177:         ratio: total > 0 ? Math.round((embedded / total) * 1_000_000) / 1_000_000 : 0,
178:       },
179:       dimMismatch,
180:       lastRefresh,
181:       disabledReason,
182:       laneEnabled: Boolean(hasActive && adapter && !dimMismatch && hasVecTable && embedded > 0 && !disabledReason),
183:       checkedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
184:     };
185:   } catch {
186:     return baseSemanticLaneHealth(runtime.disabledReason ?? 'health_unavailable');
187:   } finally {
188:     database?.close();
189:   }
190: }
191: 
192: function scanSkillMetadataFiles(
193:   skillRoot: string,
194:   maxFiles = DEFAULT_MAX_METADATA_FILES,
195: ): { count: number; maxMtimeMs: number; truncated: boolean } {
196:   if (!existsSync(skillRoot)) return { count: 0, maxMtimeMs: 0, truncated: false };
197:   const pending = [skillRoot];
198:   let count = 0;
199:   let newest = 0;
200:   let truncated = false;
201:   while (pending.length > 0) {
202:     const current = pending.pop();
203:     if (!current) continue;
204:     for (const entry of readdirSync(current, { withFileTypes: true })) {
205:       const entryPath = join(current, entry.name);
206:       if (entry.isDirectory()) {
207:         pending.push(entryPath);
208:       } else if (entry.isFile() && entry.name === 'graph-metadata.json') {
209:         count += 1;
210:         newest = Math.max(newest, statSync(entryPath).mtimeMs);
211:         if (count >= maxFiles) {
212:           truncated = true;
213:           return { count, maxMtimeMs: newest, truncated };
214:         }
215:       }
216:     }
217:   }
218:   return { count, maxMtimeMs: newest, truncated };
219: }
220: 
221: /** Read advisor freshness state.
222:  *
223:  * Diagnostic-only: this function reports stale, absent, or unavailable advisor
224:  * state and does not rebuild it. Use `advisor_rebuild` to repair stale state.
225:  */
226: export function readAdvisorStatus(input: AdvisorStatusInput): AdvisorStatusOutput {
227:   const args = AdvisorStatusInputSchema.parse(input);
228:   const workspaceRoot = resolve(args.workspaceRoot);
229:   const dbPath = resolveSkillGraphDbPath(workspaceRoot);
230:   const skillRoot = join(workspaceRoot, SKILL_ROOT);
231:   const errors: string[] = [];
232: 
233:   try {
234:     const generation = readSkillGraphGeneration(workspaceRoot);
235:     const hasSources = existsSync(skillRoot);
236:     const hasArtifact = existsSync(dbPath);
237:     // Existence alone lets a corrupt-on-disk artifact report 'live' from the
238:     // generation counters, which makes advisor_rebuild skip the repair it
239:     // exists to perform. A read-only quick_check turns genuine corruption
240:     // into a stale signal so rebuild runs. Transient lock/IO failures are
241:     // excluded so contention never forces a spurious rebuild.
242:     const integrity = (hasArtifact && args.checkArtifactIntegrity === true)
243:       ? checkSqliteIntegrityCached(dbPath, generation.generation, fileMtimeMs(dbPath))
244:       : null;
245:     const artifactCorrupt = Boolean(
246:       integrity && !integrity.ok
247:         && integrity.reason !== 'SQLITE_ABSENT'
248:         && isGenuineCorruptionReason(integrity.reason),
249:     );
250:     if (artifactCorrupt && integrity && !integrity.ok) {
251:       errors.push(`advisor_status skill graph integrity check failed (${integrity.reason}); run advisor_rebuild`);
252:     }
253:     const sourceScan = scanSkillMetadataFiles(skillRoot, args.maxMetadataFiles ?? DEFAULT_MAX_METADATA_FILES);
254:     if (sourceScan.truncated) {
255:       errors.push(`advisor_status metadata scan capped at ${sourceScan.count} files`);
256:     }
257:     const sourceChanged = generation.state === 'stale'

261:           : hasArtifact && hasSources && sourceScan.maxMtimeMs > fileMtimeMs(dbPath)

263:     const daemonPid = parseDaemonPid();
264:     const daemonAvailable = daemonPid === undefined ? true : hasRunningDaemon(daemonPid);
265:     // Only flag a daemon-evidence divergence when a PID file exists but the
266:     // process is gone. Missing PID file = daemon never started, not a
267:     // divergence between freshness artifacts and live process evidence.
268:     if (daemonPid !== undefined && !daemonAvailable && generation.state !== 'unavailable') {
269:       errors.push('advisor_status found freshness artifacts, but no live daemon PID/process evidence');
270:     }
271:     const state = isFreshness(generation.state) ? generation.state : 'unavailable';
272:     // Data is stale when sources are physically newer than the
273:     // artifact, regardless of stored sourceSignature semantics. This is the
274:     // stronger physical-evidence signal vs. signature mismatch which can
275:     // arise from benign workspace differences.
276:     const sourcesNewerThanArtifact = hasArtifact && hasSources
277:       && sourceScan.maxMtimeMs > fileMtimeMs(dbPath);
278:     const trustState = createTrustState({
279:       hasSources,
280:       hasArtifact,
281:       sourceChanged,
282:       daemonAvailable,
283:       generation: generation.generation,
284:       reason: generation.reason,
285:       lastLiveAt: state === 'live' ? generation.updatedAt : null,
286:     });
287:     // `freshness` describes data/artifact state (live/stale/absent/
288:     // unavailable from the generation file), while `trustState.state` is the
289:     // daemon-gated caller-trust axis. Keep them decoupled so callers reading
290:     // `freshness` see artifact health independent of daemon availability.
291:     // When physical evidence shows sources are newer than the DB, downgrade
292:     // a 'live' generation to 'stale' regardless of signature noise.
293:     const freshnessOutput: AdvisorFreshness = state === 'live'
294:       && (sourceChanged || artifactCorrupt || (!generation.sourceSignature && sourcesNewerThanArtifact))
295:       ? 'stale'
296:       : state;
297:     const output: AdvisorStatusOutput = {
298:       freshness: freshnessOutput,
299:       generation: generation.generation,
300:       trustState,
301:       lastGenerationBump: generation.updatedAt === new Date(0).toISOString() ? null : generation.updatedAt,
302:       lastScanAt: generation.updatedAt === new Date(0).toISOString() ? null : generation.updatedAt,
303:       skillCount: sourceScan.count,
304:       laneWeights: DEFAULT_SCORER_WEIGHTS,
305:       ...((args.includeSemanticHealth || args.debug) ? { semanticLaneHealth: readSemanticLaneHealth(dbPath) } : {}),
306:       ...(daemonPid ? { daemonPid } : {}),
307:       ...(errors.length > 0 ? { errors } : {}),
308:     };
309:     return AdvisorStatusOutputSchema.parse(output);
310:   } catch (error: unknown) {
311:     const message = redactDiagnosticText(errorMessage(error));
312:     const sourceScan = scanSkillMetadataFiles(skillRoot, args.maxMetadataFiles ?? DEFAULT_MAX_METADATA_FILES);
313:     const trustState = createTrustState({
314:       hasSources: existsSync(skillRoot),
315:       hasArtifact: existsSync(dbPath),
316:       sourceChanged: false,
317:       daemonAvailable: false,
318:       generation: 0,
319:       reason: 'ADVISOR_STATUS_UNAVAILABLE',
320:     });
321:     const output: AdvisorStatusOutput = {
322:       freshness: 'unavailable',
323:       generation: 0,
324:       trustState,
325:       lastGenerationBump: null,
326:       lastScanAt: null,
327:       skillCount: sourceScan.count,
328:       laneWeights: DEFAULT_SCORER_WEIGHTS,
329:       ...((args.includeSemanticHealth || args.debug) ? { semanticLaneHealth: readSemanticLaneHealth(dbPath) } : {}),
330:       errors: [message],
331:     };
332:     return AdvisorStatusOutputSchema.parse(output);
333:   }
334: }
335: 
336: /** Handle the advisor_status MCP tool request. */
337: export async function handleAdvisorStatus(args: unknown): Promise<HandlerResponse> {
338:   // The diagnostic surface opts into the artifact integrity probe by default
339:   // so operators see on-disk corruption; explicit input can still override it.
340:   const parsed = AdvisorStatusInputSchema.parse(args);
341:   const data = readAdvisorStatus({ ...parsed, checkArtifactIntegrity: parsed.checkArtifactIntegrity ?? true });
342:   return {
343:     content: [{
344:       type: 'text',
345:       text: JSON.stringify({ status: 'ok', data }, null, 2),
346:     }],
347:   };
348: }
349: 
350: /** Backward-compatible snake_case MCP handler alias. */
351: export const handle_advisor_status = handleAdvisorStatus;

159: function evidenceFeature(value: string): string {

164:     case 'token':
165:       return 'token_match';
166:     case 'hint':

168:     case 'derived':
169:       return 'derived_skill_signal';
170:     case 'doc':
171:       return 'doc_reference_signal';
172:     case 'cosine':
173:       return 'semantic_similarity';
174:     default:
175:       if (value.includes('disambiguation')) return 'disambiguation_rule';
176:       if (value.includes('surface')) return 'surface_signal';
177:       if (value.includes('intent')) return 'intent_signal';
178:       if (value.includes('loop')) return 'workflow_signal';

183: function publicWhyRecommended(recommendation: ScoredRecommendation) {
184:   const positiveLanes = recommendation.laneContributions
185:     .filter((lane) => lane.rawScore > 0 || lane.weightedScore > 0)
186:     .sort((left, right) => right.weightedScore - left.weightedScore)
187:     .slice(0, 4);
188:   const matchedSkillFeatures = positiveLanes.flatMap((lane) => (
189:     Array.from(new Set(lane.evidence.map((entry) => evidenceFeature(entry)))).map((feature) => ({
190:       lane: lane.lane,
191:       feature,
192:     }))
193:   ));
194:   const dominant = recommendation.dominantLane;
195:   return {
196:     reason: dominant
197:       ? `Recommended from ranker signals: ${dominant} contributed the strongest live score.`
198:       : 'Recommended from ranker signals without a single dominant live lane.',
199:     dominantLane: dominant,
200:     topLanes: positiveLanes.map((lane) => ({
201:       lane: lane.lane,
202:       rawScore: lane.rawScore,
203:       weightedScore: lane.weightedScore,
204:       weight: lane.weight,
205:       shadowOnly: lane.shadowOnly,
206:       evidenceTypes: Array.from(new Set(lane.evidence.map((entry) => evidenceFeature(entry)))),
207:       evidenceCount: lane.evidence.length,
208:     })),
209:     matchedSkillFeatures,
210:   };
211: }

213: // Doc evidence entries are skill-relative markdown paths emitted by the

216: const SAFE_DOC_PATH = /^(references|assets)\/[A-Za-z0-9_./-]+\.md$/;

218: export function matchedDocsFromContributions(

220: ): string[] {

222:   for (const lane of recommendation.laneContributions) {

225:       const path = entry.slice(4);

227:       if (!docs.includes(path)) docs.push(path);
228:       if (docs.length >= 3) return docs;
229:     }
230:   }
231:   return docs;

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Skill Doc Frontmatter Harvest
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Parses the canonical reference/asset frontmatter contract
5: // (title, description, trigger_phrases, importance_tier, contextType)
6: // so per-doc trigger phrases can feed advisor routing as doc-level
7: // signal. The Spec Kit Memory system deliberately never indexes these
8: // files; the advisor is their only runtime consumer.
9:

11: import { join } from 'node:path';
12: 
13: export interface ParsedDocFrontmatter {
14:   readonly title: string;
15:   readonly description: string;
16:   readonly triggerPhrases: readonly string[];
17:   readonly importanceTier: string;
18:   readonly contextType: string;
19: }
20: 
21: /** Opt-in gate for the whole doc-harvest path. Default off. */
22: export function isDocTriggerHarvestEnabled(env: NodeJS.ProcessEnv = process.env): boolean {
23:   return env.SPECKIT_ADVISOR_DOC_TRIGGERS?.trim().toLowerCase() === 'true';
24: }
25: 
26: // Mirrors the spec-kit importance vocabulary so doc authors reuse one
27: // tier language. Values dampen routing contribution, never boost above
28: // the skill's own curated signal.
29: const DOC_TIER_WEIGHTS: Readonly<Record<string, number>> = {
30:   constitutional: 1.0,
31:   critical: 1.0,
32:   important: 0.85,
33:   normal: 0.7,
34:   temporary: 0.4,
35:   deprecated: 0.2,
36: };
37: 
38: const DEFAULT_TIER = 'normal';
39: 
40: export function docTierWeight(tier: string | null | undefined): number {
41:   const normalized = (tier ?? DEFAULT_TIER).trim().toLowerCase();
42:   return DOC_TIER_WEIGHTS[normalized] ?? DOC_TIER_WEIGHTS[DEFAULT_TIER];
43: }
44: 
45: const SURROUNDING_QUOTES = /^["']|["']$/g;
46: const KEY_LINE = /^([A-Za-z0-9_-]+):\s*(.*)$/;
47: const LIST_ITEM = /^\s+-\s+(.*)$/;
48: const MAX_PHRASES_PER_DOC = 12;
49: const MAX_FIELD_LENGTH = 300;
50: 
51: function cleanScalar(raw: string): string {
52:   return raw.trim().replace(SURROUNDING_QUOTES, '').trim().slice(0, MAX_FIELD_LENGTH);
53: }

55: function splitInlineList(inner: string): string[] {
56:   const entries: string[] = [];
57:   let current = '';
58:   let quote: '"' | "'" | null = null;
59:   let escaped = false;
60:   for (const char of inner) {
61:     if (escaped) {
62:       current += char;
63:       escaped = false;
64:       continue;
65:     }
66:     if (char === '\\' && quote) {
67:       current += char;
68:       escaped = true;
69:       continue;
70:     }
71:     if ((char === '"' || char === "'") && (!quote || quote === char)) {
72:       quote = quote ? null : char;
73:       current += char;
74:       continue;
75:     }
76:     if (char === ',' && !quote) {
77:       entries.push(current);
78:       current = '';
79:       continue;
80:     }
81:     current += char;
82:   }
83:   if (current.trim()) entries.push(current);
84:   return entries;
85: }

87: /**
88:  * Parse the YAML-ish frontmatter block of a skill reference/asset doc.
89:  *
90:  * Returns null when the doc has no frontmatter fence or no
91:  * `trigger_phrases` entries — docs without the detailed contract carry
92:  * no doc-level routing signal and are skipped, not errored.
93:  */
94: export function parseDocFrontmatter(raw: string): ParsedDocFrontmatter | null {
95:   if (!raw.startsWith('---\n') && !raw.startsWith('---\r\n')) return null;
96:   const end = raw.indexOf('\n---', 3);
97:   if (end <= 3) return null;
98:   const block = raw.slice(raw.indexOf('\n') + 1, end);
99: 
100:   const scalars: Record<string, string> = {};
101:   const phrases: string[] = [];
102:   let inPhraseList = false;
103: 
104:   for (const line of block.split(/\r?\n/)) {
105:     const listMatch = LIST_ITEM.exec(line);
106:     if (listMatch) {
107:       if (inPhraseList && phrases.length < MAX_PHRASES_PER_DOC) {
108:         const phrase = cleanScalar(listMatch[1]);
109:         if (phrase) phrases.push(phrase);
110:       }
111:       continue;
112:     }
113: 
114:     const keyMatch = KEY_LINE.exec(line);
115:     if (!keyMatch) continue;
116:     const key = keyMatch[1];
117:     const value = keyMatch[2].trim();
118:     inPhraseList = key === 'trigger_phrases';
119: 
120:     if (key === 'trigger_phrases' && value.startsWith('[')) {
121:       // Inline list form: trigger_phrases: ["a", "b"]
122:       const inner = value.replace(/^\[|\]$/g, '');
123:       for (const entry of splitInlineList(inner)) {
124:         if (phrases.length >= MAX_PHRASES_PER_DOC) break;
125:         const phrase = cleanScalar(entry);
126:         if (phrase) phrases.push(phrase);
127:       }
128:       inPhraseList = false;
129:       continue;
130:     }
131: 
132:     if (value) scalars[key] = cleanScalar(value);
133:   }
134: 
135:   if (phrases.length === 0) return null;
136: 
137:   return {
138:     title: scalars.title ?? '',
139:     description: scalars.description ?? '',
140:     triggerPhrases: phrases,
141:     importanceTier: (scalars.importance_tier ?? DEFAULT_TIER).toLowerCase(),
142:     contextType: (scalars.contextType ?? scalars.context_type ?? 'general').toLowerCase(),
143:   };
144: }
145: 
146: const HARVEST_SUBDIRS = ['references', 'assets'] as const;
147: const MAX_WALK_DEPTH = 6;
148: const MAX_DOCS_PER_SKILL = 200;
149: 
150: function isHarvestableMarkdown(name: string): boolean {
151:   const lower = name.toLowerCase();
152:   return lower.endsWith('.md') && lower !== 'readme.md';
153: }
154: 
155: function walkDocsDir(dir: string, depth: number, collected: string[]): void {
156:   if (depth > MAX_WALK_DEPTH || collected.length >= MAX_DOCS_PER_SKILL) return;
157:   let entries;
158:   try {
159:     entries = readdirSync(dir, { withFileTypes: true });
160:   } catch {
161:     return;
162:   }
163:   for (const entry of entries) {
164:     if (collected.length >= MAX_DOCS_PER_SKILL) return;
165:     const fullPath = join(dir, entry.name);
166:     if (entry.isDirectory()) {
167:       walkDocsDir(fullPath, depth + 1, collected);
168:       continue;
169:     }
170:     if (!entry.isFile() || !isHarvestableMarkdown(entry.name)) continue;
171:     collected.push(fullPath);
172:   }
173: }
174: 
175: /**
176:  * List harvestable doc files (references/assets markdown, READMEs
177:  * excluded) for one skill directory. Paths are absolute and sorted for
178:  * deterministic indexing.
179:  */
180: export function listSkillDocFiles(skillDir: string): string[] {
181:   const collected: string[] = [];
182:   for (const subdir of HARVEST_SUBDIRS) {
183:     const root = join(skillDir, subdir);
184:     try {
185:       if (!statSync(root).isDirectory()) continue;
186:     } catch {
187:       continue;
188:     }
189:     walkDocsDir(root, 0, collected);
190:   }
191:   return collected.sort();
192: }

(End of file - total 192 lines)
</content>

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Advisor Skill Alias Groups
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: 
5: const RAW_ALIAS_GROUPS: Readonly<Record<string, readonly string[]>> = Object.freeze({
6:   'create:agent': ['command-create-agent', '/create:agent', 'create:agent'],
7:   'create:testing-playbook': [
8:     'command-create-testing-playbook',
9:     '/create:testing-playbook',
10:     'create:testing-playbook',
11:   ],
12:   'memory:save': ['command-memory-save', '/memory:save', 'memory:save'],
13:   'deep-research': [
14:     'command-spec-kit-deep-research',
15:     '/deep:start-research-loop',
16:     'spec_kit:deep-research',
17:     'deep-research',
18:     'sk-deep-research',
19:   ],
20:   'deep-review': [
21:     'command-spec-kit-deep-review',
22:     '/deep:start-review-loop',
23:     'spec_kit:deep-review',
24:     'deep-review',
25:     'sk-deep-review',
26:   ],
27:   'deep-improvement': [
28:     'command-spec-kit-deep-agent-improvement',
29:     '/deep:start-agent-improvement-loop',
30:     'deep-agent-improvement',
31:     'sk-deep-agent-improvement',
32:   ],
33:   'deep-model-benchmark': [
34:     'command-spec-kit-deep-model-benchmark',
35:     '/deep:start-model-benchmark-loop',
36:     'deep-model-benchmark',
37:     'sk-deep-model-benchmark',
38:   ],
39:   'deep-ai-council': [
40:     '@deep-ai-council',
41:     'deep-ai-council',
42:     'deep ai council',
43:     'ai council',
44:     'planning council',
45:     'council deliberation',
46:     'multi-ai-council',
47:   ],
48: });
49: 
50: export const SKILL_ALIAS_GROUPS: Readonly<Record<string, readonly string[]>> = RAW_ALIAS_GROUPS;
51: 
52: const ALIAS_TO_CANONICAL = new Map<string, string>(
53:   Object.entries(RAW_ALIAS_GROUPS).flatMap(([canonical, aliases]) => [
54:     [canonical, canonical] as const,
55:     ...aliases.map((alias) => [alias, canonical] as const),
56:   ]),
57: );
58: 
59: export function canonicalSkillId(skillId: string): string {
60:   return ALIAS_TO_CANONICAL.get(skillId) ?? skillId;
61: }
62: 
63: export function skillMatchesAlias(actual: string, expected: string): boolean {
64:   return canonicalSkillId(actual) === canonicalSkillId(expected);
65: }
66: 
67: export function skillInAliasSet(actual: string, expected: readonly string[]): boolean {
68:   return expected.some((candidate) => skillMatchesAlias(actual, candidate));
69: }
70: 
71: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
72: // MODULE: Merged Deep-Loop Identity + Mode Layer
73: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
74: //
75: // The five legacy deep-loop skills (deep-context, deep-research, deep-review,
76: // deep-ai-council, deep-improvement) are folded into one public skill,
77: // deep-loop-workflows, discriminated by workflowMode (see
78: // deep-loop-workflows/mode-registry.json). canonicalSkillId above is
79: // deliberately UNCHANGED: an alias still resolves to its mode-level legacy id
80: // (e.g. 'spec_kit:deep-review' -> 'deep-review') so existing callers and the
81: // alias contract keep working. This layer adds the SECOND projection — legacy
82: // mode-level id -> (merged skill, workflowMode) — that routers use to land on
83: // deep-loop-workflows with the right mode once the old skill nodes leave the
84: // graph.
85: //
86: // deep-context is intentionally absent from DEEP_MODE_BY_CANONICAL: it has no
87: // alias group here and stays metadata-routed (resolved from its
88: // graph-metadata.json), mirroring the Python advisor where DEEP_ROUTING_SKILLS
89: // excludes it. Its workflowMode ('context') still lives in the mode registry.
90: export const MERGED_DEEP_SKILL_ID = 'deep-loop-workflows';
91: 
92: // Legacy mode-level skill id -> registry workflowMode. deep-improvement maps to
93: // its Lane A default workflowMode ('agent-improvement'); the other improvement
94: // sub-lanes (model-benchmark, skill-benchmark, non-dev-ai-system-refine) keep
95: // their own command bridges and are not folded through this alias map.
96: export const DEEP_MODE_BY_CANONICAL: Readonly<Record<string, string>> = Object.freeze({
97:   'deep-research': 'research',
98:   'deep-review': 'review',
99:   'deep-ai-council': 'ai-council',
100:   'deep-improvement': 'agent-improvement',
101: });
102: 
103: const ALIAS_TO_MODE = new Map<string, string>(
104:   Object.entries(RAW_ALIAS_GROUPS).flatMap(([canonical, aliases]) => {
105:     const mode = DEEP_MODE_BY_CANONICAL[canonical];
106:     if (!mode) return [];
107:     return [
108:       [canonical, mode] as const,
109:       ...aliases.map((alias) => [alias, mode] as const),
110:     ];
111:   }),
112: );
113: 
114: /**
115:  * Resolve any deep-loop alias (or mode-level legacy id) to its workflowMode, or
116:  * null when the id is not a folded deep-loop mode. deep-context returns null by
117:  * design (metadata-routed; not represented in the alias groups).
118:  */
119: export function modeForAlias(skillId: string): string | null {
120:   return ALIAS_TO_MODE.get(skillId) ?? ALIAS_TO_MODE.get(canonicalSkillId(skillId)) ?? null;
121: }
122: 
123: /**
124:  * Resolve any deep-loop alias to the merged skill id (deep-loop-workflows) when
125:  * it names a folded mode, else fall back to the plain canonical id. Non-deep
126:  * skills are returned unchanged.
127:  */
128: export function mergedSkillForAlias(skillId: string): string {
129:   return modeForAlias(skillId) !== null ? MERGED_DEEP_SKILL_ID : canonicalSkillId(skillId);
130: }
